entry.title: Unlocking Continuous Quality Checks (CQCs) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Are you an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition customer and interested in requesting and learning more about how the available CQC services can support your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition solution? If so, then I invite you list to our below E-learning.

  • Unlocking Continuous Quality Checks (CQCs) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

(Before you can access the link, a one-time registration in the SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required. A detailed step-by-step guide on how to register can be found here.)

Service Overview:

ServiceAimWhen to UseAdditional Information
CQC for Transformation AssessmentGauge/provide feedback on your project team’s awareness of topics and resources that are critical to project success

Prepare session in prepare phase

Realize session in the realize phase

  • Information sheet
  • Roadmap Viewer
CQC for Deployment ReadinessPre go-live SAP best practice review of key SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition settings

Two months prior to go live

  • Information sheet
  • Roadmap Viewer
CQC for Process Adoption

Service will review the usage of your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition solution and help you improve current business processes and adopt new business processes.

Run phase at least 6 months after go-live
  • Information sheet
  • Roadmap Viewer
CQC for Data Volume Management

Review the solution under data volume management and information lifecycle management aspects

Run phase at least 6 months after go-liveIn development – if interested please reach out to your SAP Contact

All CQCs listed above are included as part of your SAP Enterprise Support cloud edition support maintenance contract which is embedded in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition subscription with no additional costs. Please note you can also find a complied list of our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition learnings here - SAP Enterprise Support Learning Assets for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Meet-the-Expert Sessions and E-Learnings.

In addition, please sign up for the value map for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to receive full guidance on CQCs and get personalized guidance on your cloud ERP journey. In the value map, you can collaborate in forums, ask questions, or use the “Request a Call” feature to speak with an expert. To learn more about value maps, watch the following video.

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK: How to R/W values from/to editable DataTable...
entry.content:
Can you please provide more details on where / what you see in the Mentor App?

You can access the changes using the getChanges API method.  You may want to review the What's New blog when we introduced the feature in MDK 23.3

entry.title: Can we create BP without Vendor/Customer role in S/4 using LTMC?
entry.content: Can we create BP without Vendor role or Customer role using any LTMC templates? Need to load BP without customer role but need to load Sales data and Company code data. Is it technically possible to load Sales and Company data using Customer Master LTMC template?

entry.title: Re: Background Data is updated in Candidate Profile but n...
entry.content:
Hi @Gi ,

PFB note from implementation guide:

Problem Stmt: There are few fields on candidate profile which are not visible to Quick Apply applicant. And at later stage the candidate is entering the data on candidate profile but same is not helping to reflect it over job application.

 

Proposed Solution:
1) Create a new dedicated JRDM and Job Application template for Quick Apply

2) Include the affected fields in newly created Job Application Template

3) Hide the newly included fields from step 2 using Multi-stage Application

4) Identify the point/status from the talent pipeline to ask for input from candidate against fields included in step 2

5) Make the fields visible to Candidate and Recruiter at this stage

 

I hope this approach will be helpful for you.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Errors on revision date in curriculum connector
entry.content: tried with one sample directly into notepad but it still adding a 0

entry.title: Are you familiar with single level cost estimates?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Introducing Product Cost Planning and Production Accounting in SAP S/HANA learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey will guide business users through understanding and updating the standard price of a product, while familiarizing themselves with single level cost estimates. This learning journeys addresses learners who are interested in SAP S/4HANA Cloud and on premise deployment options. 

Learning objectives
Upon completing this learning journey, business users will be able to understand and apply various cost estimation techniques, including evaluating item categories and technical quantity structures, utilizing master data, and accurately valuating and releasing standard prices within a business environment.

Prerequisites

  • Outlining Cost Management and Profitability Analysis in SAP S/4HANA
  • Performing Overhead Cost Controlling​ in SAP S/4HANA

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Managing Multiple Material Numbers in SAP S/4HANA Clo...
entry.content: Perform some search on EINA-IDNLF 'Material Number Used by Vendor'

entry.title: Re: Create Variable for 0HIER_VERS
entry.content: It's been a while but i think the solution was to create the variable in Eclipse

entry.title: Re: Can you remember the first time you heard of SAP
entry.content: Hi Ruthvik, I first heard of SAP while working at Storage Technology Corporation, in the mid 90s!  I was working at Storage as a Field Support Analyst for Service Billing on the Tape Library's we sold, and they decided to replace their home grown legacy systems with SAP 3.1H.  Turned out SAP released Service Management with 3.1 H and I was invited to join the program and learn how to implement Order to Cash and Service Management!  It was the beginning of my long association with SAP.  I worked for the mother ship from 2003 until 2017!  I now help SAP customers, locally, as an SAP Testing Project Manager!

entry.title: Skip bin section RF scan from storage bin field in EWM
entry.content:
Hi gurus,

There is the possibility to skip the bin section (storage behavior: Pallet) counter from the storage bin scan in RF?

In the image we see that the main bin is the 110201 and the subbin is the 110201/1, where the bin section is represented with "/1", this counter can be avoided and only scan the main bin (110201) directly from RF scan?

Mainly because the customer doesn't want to relabel the storage bins from the warehouse.

Thanks

Best regards

entry.title: Re: User is trying to post unplanned dep should post with...
entry.content: Check your Customizing : Asset Accounting, Depreciation, Unplanned Depreciation, Define Transaction Types for Unplanned Depreciation, Define /and/ Limit to Depreciation Areas

entry.title: Re: Working with RAP BO Events end-to-end - Event-driven-architecture - Event framework
entry.content:
Local Events is one of the most powerful tools I've seen in SAP in a long time. More over if we combine it with BALI Logs to track the behavior of the code triggered by the event.

It's a shame that SAP doesn't have an app to display the logs in Cloud Public, just like we have done during decades with tcode SLG1: https://community.sap.com/t5/general-and-cross-topics-q-a/is-it-possible-to-display-custom-application-logs-in-s-4hana-cloud-public/qaq-p/14008266

entry.title: Re: Stay certified - Is an SAP Learning Hub subscription...
entry.content:
Hi @mb1011 (Mihael)
Pease follow these  steps to stay certified

1. Get an SAP Universal ID
Make sure you have a valid SAP Universal ID with at least two email accounts assigned to it. If you don’t, watch the video for instructions.
2. Ensure you have an SAP Learning Hub subscription to access the assessment to stay certified
If you have no subscription, you’ll need to book and retake the full 2-3 hour SAP Certification exam every year. Check your subscription or subscribe to SAP Learning Hub.
3. Check your certification status
Log in to My Learning to see your certifications and their expiration dates.
Make sure you complete the short annual assessment as soon as possible to extend the validity of your certification by 12 months. 
If the certification has been retired and you want to retain your certified status, you need to re-certify in an alternative certification. Find recommended alternatives using the SAP Certification finder

4. Prepare for the assessment
Find preparation courses or live sessions linked under your certification.

5. Take the assessment
The assessment is a short, non-proctored quiz with unlimited attempts. Once you’ve completed it successfully, the validity of your certification will be extended by 12 months.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Get Discovered as a Supplier on SAP Business Network
entry.content:
With over $69 billion in business opportunities posted on the network last year, there is no shortage of potential for suppliers to get discovered by global buyers and grow their business. You can get started building your presence on SAP Business Network today, whether you have zero or many customers on there today. 

Optimizing your profile for better leads  

As buyers search for products and services on SAP Business Network, information from supplier company profiles, in addition to network catalog content, is used to populate the search results. By having a complete, and up to date profile, you can optimize your chances of generating leads.  

In particular, the following criteria from company profiles is used in buyer searches:  

  • Company name- Identify who you are to buyers  
  • Certificates- Build trust and credibility by demonstrating your commitment to quality standards, expertise in your field, adherence to industry best practices, etc.  
  • Keywords- Increase searchability when a buyer search matches your keywords  
  • Ship to/service locations- Show buyers if they are in your service range  
  • Product and service categories- Help potential customers understand your offerings and increase your searchability  

Check out this helpful checklist to make sure you fully complete your profile and increase your chances of receiving leads from interested buyers.  

Generating leads with a network catalog  

A network catalog is a single master catalog that can be created via file upload. It is searchable by keyword and viewable by all buyers on SAP Business Network. Companies can include both products and services, regardless of how they are measured, priced, or delivered, in their catalog. Each catalog item includes fields for a name, description, photo, specs, price, and a link to the uploading supplier’s company profile.  

What are the benefits?  

  • Uploading a network catalog provides increased visibility for the company and their offerings, as well as high quality lead generation.   
  • By uploading a searchable network catalog, suppliers can share their entire catalog with all buyers on SAP Business Network.   
  • This allows suppliers to be able to upload and maintain all content in one source.  
  • These buyers are ready to buy the products and services offered by the supplier as they are searching for specific content on the network.   
  • Once a buyer finds a product or service they are interested in, they can send a request for information (RFI) via the price check button on the product listing, which will show up in the supplier’s leads dashboard within the portal.  

Maximize your potential for leads  

Launching in February 2025, the SAP Business Network, promote subscription will offer tools designed to fully maximize your potential for leads as a network supplier, such as unlimited network catalog publishing edits, open APIs, GenAI capabilities, profile verification, insights and more. It will be available for purchase in the portal. Visit this page to learn more about promote.  

Complete Your Profile and Upload Your Catalog Today  

If you don’t already have an account, you can register for a free SAP Business Network, standard account in just a few minutes, or log in if you already have an account to get started. All standard and enterprise accounts will be able to upload a network via file upload with a limit of 1000 publishing edits. For unlimited publishing capabilities and additional catalog tools, suppliers can purchase the promote subscription directly in the portal. 

Register or login to complete your profile and catalog today.

entry.title: Message no. FF707 SAP, is a warning message
entry.content:
The message no. FF707 SAP, is a warning message but when the amount is 0, it writes two rows in the BSET table:
1. one with zero vat amount
1. one with the correct value

does anyone know how to solve? i.e. not to have the double row written in the BSET?

entry.title: Re: Which course should i take to clear the fiori develop...
entry.content:
Hi @dasarijit 

Please access our C_FIORD SAP Certified Associate - SAP Fiori Application Developer certification site.
There you can find the How to prepare section and sample question for self-evaluation.

Please access the C_FIOAD SAP Certified Associate - SAP Fiori System Administration  certification site.
there you can find the How to prepare  and sample question for self-evaluation.

All the best
Margit

entry.title: Re: Assign multiple valuation methodes in FCV to one Vali...
entry.content:
Hello Felix,

Within a rule it is possible to define more than one valuation rule step.

Example:

You have a rule called ABCD.

Within the rule you define a valuation rule step 10 and a valuation rule step 20.

To the step 10 you can assign the FX semantic tag and the method always valuate.

To the rule step 20, you can assign the FX2 semantic tag and the method lower cost or market principle, as reuquired.

I hope this answers you question.

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: Message no. FF707 SAP, is a warning message
entry.content:
Did you considert switching back the message to an error ?

  • Reverse of 3422955 - Error FF707 for FI Posting transactions.
  • Other solutions in 3533781 - FF707 for FB01 or BAPI / Customizing or BAdI 2449228 - Changing message FF707 using BAdI or Increase the tolerance.

entry.title: IAS Custom Schema with Multivalue Field Propagation to SAP CAP
entry.content:
Hello experts!

We are using a Custom Schema from SAP Cloud Identity Service to indicate user data, such as the centers the user has access to, to restrict data access for a CAP application.

We are propagating this in the Cloud Identity Service application to be consumed both in a UI5 application, via a request to the sap-approuter-userapi service, and to restrict data access in CAP with the @restrict annotation.

In Cloud Identity Service, we parameterize users as follows:

The problem is that, instead of seeing an array of strings in the API, we are seeing a string with the array value inside.

In the request to the sap-approuter-userapi service, at the attributes endpoint, we get:

When we expected to get an array of strings as in the scopes or Groups properties.

At CAP filters level, we have:

And debugging the filters, the value that arrives in the read implementation would be the following:

This causes the restrict not to work as it filters by the string and not by the N values we parameterized in SAP Cloud Identity Service.

In the attribute type configuration in xs-security.json, we have defined it as “s” because we have not found in the documentation how to define it as an object or an array of strings.

We have found a workaround both in UI5 and in the CAP implementations of the “on” READ to transform that string into an array of strings and create the restrict filters object, but we believe the behavior should be that both the sap-approuter-userapi service API and the automatic implementation in CAP of the user parameters should return an array of strings.

Is this the standard behaviour of custom schema attributes marked as multivalue or are we missing some configuration?

Thank you in advance for your help,

Alejandro.

entry.title: Re: MTD, YTD, YTG (YearToGo) Calculations in SAP Analytics Cloud

Actually, the question was referring only to the SAP Could Excel Add-in. 
I am familiar with YTD calculations in SAP Cloud.

entry.title: PM: Why aren't additional resources visible on the screen when I add a new one to the team section?
entry.content:
Hello experts,

While creating a customer project in Project Management, I attempted to add a new additional resource under the Team section, but the additional resource area did not appear on the screen. I checked the IMG settings and product master data and they were completed. Additionally, the expense area has also disappeared.

I am not sure what else to check to resolve this issue. Please advise. Thank you for your help,

Wendy

entry.title: Re: Reimagining digital learning as an integrated part of a Digital Adoption Platform with SAP &&&&
entry.content:
@lben 

The general availability of the new WalkMe offering has not yet been announced, so it cannot be delayed. 

A new SAP Enable Now edition focused on digital learning is planned as an interim solution for new customers until the new WalkMe offering is available.

entry.title: Re: fill Component list from maintenance plans work orders
entry.content:
Hello @JoshCotton ,

You can do it in task list operation screen where you will find the component button. 

Once you click the component button, you will navigate to the component tab where you can assign the spare parts.

 

Save it. Once you assign the task list to the maintenance plan and trigger the order, the components (spare parts) will be copied to maintenance order.

Vaibhav

entry.title: Re: Value Accelerator Event - Structure question
entry.content: Hello Fabian thank you for your answer. Which kind of confirm what I understood. What I am not clear of though : is why do we need a timestamp. Indeed if my invoice is due today it will be overdue tomorrow (D+1) : no need to set a 23:59:59 time stamp for that ! Have a nice day. Sanddie

entry.title: Re: Input control unexpected behavior SAC
entry.content:
Hi @Deb_D,

As you mentioned earlier you have no data in model 

so make sure that in input control (Filter) setting select "Unbooked members" On 

Kindly upvote if you find it helpful.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK: How to R/W values from/to editable DataTable...
entry.content:
Thank you for answering so quickly.
Unfortunately, we are not able to retrieve changes using:

 

clientAPI.getControl('SectionedTable0').getSection('SectionDataTable0').getChanges();

// or:

clientAPI.getPageProxy().getControl('SectionedTable0').getSection('SectionDataTable0').getChanges();

 

Depending on whether we use getPageProxy() or not we either get an empty array or an empty object.
And the Mentor app example I was referring to is the Mentor demo app of the client app. In there, the data table showcase has our desired functionality.

entry.title: Re: Mongo ODBC Connection - failing to connect, Crystal 2...
entry.content: No joy on connecting without using the CR wizard. CR crashes before even connecting to Atlas. It appears it is crashing because it is trying to access restricted memory or something similar when loading the driver. Also, it is a bit strange that CR asks for login information, instead of just using the info in the ODBC config as is. CR isn't even fully loading the driver. (Again - important to note that other applications like MS Excel can connect without issue)

entry.title: Re: How to refresh the Feature Traceability view (and oth...
entry.content: Thanks Paula. Indeed, I will submit a an idea request for this, as a simple refresh button makes a lot of sense, if the view is not refreshing automatically. Thank you.

entry.title: Situation Handling in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public/Private Edition
entry.content:
Are you an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition or Private Edition customer?

  • Are you interested in understating how to manage critical issues efficiently in today's fast-paced environment?
  • Do you want to understand how to identify urgent business issues, such as nearing deadlines and pending confirmations?
  • Would you like to learn about the key features of Situation Handling and understand how to implement relevant use cases for your business?

If the answer is yes to any of the above, then please make sure to register for the upcoming empowerment session with our experts.

Before you can access the link below, a one-time registration in the SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required. A detailed step-by-step guide to registration can be found here.

Session Title:

Date and Time:

Registration

Situation Handling in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public/Private Edition

April 16th 2025 3:00pm – 4:00 pm GMT

Here

(Please note that the recording will also be made available via the above link, after the session has been made available).

During this live empowerment session, you will:

  • Get an overview of Situation Handling in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • Learn about Situation Handling capabilities and how to get started
  • Gain insight into the Situation Handling roadmap

entry.title: Re: API to read and set Story Filters
Are You Ready to Touch the Future of Utilities?
entry.content:
As spring approaches, so does the highly anticipated SAP for Energy and Utilities Conference 2025. The beautiful city of Rotterdam will welcome us not only with its modern architecture and vibrant harbor atmosphere, but also with a unique opportunity to literally “touch” innovation—in the truest sense of the word.

The SAP Pop-up Campus promises an unforgettable experience, blending knowledge sharing with hands-on learning. This immersive environment features a massive 180° LED screen, designed to present complex concepts and solutions in a clear, simple, and truly "touchable" way.

Prepare to discover how AI is revolutionizing SAP solutions for Utilities, ensuring companies are well-prepared for the future. The interactive format of the SAP Pop-up Campus will let you experience AI for Utilities in action: meet a service agent who works faster with AI support, and see how AI completely modernizes customer communication.

Are you curious? Spaces are limited, so hurry up! Secure your ticket via the event app as soon as registration opens and take the first step towards the next era of business innovation. You can find these presentations and more on our agenda!

Looking forward to meeting you in Rotterdam! 🚢 🌊

entry.title: Re: API to read and set Story Filters
entry.content:
@GST11 , you need to account for the Type: multiple or single.
Take a look here: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/how-to-show-the-story-filter-value-in-a-textbox-in-sac/qaq-p/14014413/comment-id/4904348

entry.title: SuccessFactors API language
entry.content:
Hi,

I've been scratching my head for a moment already. Is there a way to pass the language as a parameter or a header so the API would return the localized value in the given language ?

Say I have an API user whom default language is English (en_GB) and I'd like to get countries (Country) with the externalName_localized in French, is there any way ?

I tried the following:

  • sap-locale
  • sapml-language
  • localization
  • locale
  • sap-language
  • Accept-Language
  • Content-Language

None of the parameters or headers (for the last 2) worked.

The only way to get the data from the API in French is to change the default language in SuccessFactors directly, which is clearly not the intent.

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK: How to R/W values from/to editable DataTable...

entry.title: Re: How to add eventDelegate to control which is inside a Dialog.
entry.content: Hi. Did you find a good solution for this?

entry.title: Re: Change properties of Attachment Component on Create M...
entry.content:
I wasn't able to solve the issue using a manifest change, but I was able to change the properties with a controller extension. 

I made a new function that gets called after any batch request completes on the component. 

 

_setAttachmentServiceAttributes: function () {
	var oComponent = this.getView().byId("workRequestCreateAttachSrvCompContCreate").getComponentInstance();
	oComponent.getRootControl().getController().oModel.attachBatchRequestCompleted(function (oEvent) {
		oComponent.setFlavor("withCheckin");

		var fieldControl = oComponent.getAttributes();
		fieldControl._VisibleActions.RENAME = true;
		fieldControl._VisibleActions.DELETE = true;
		fieldControl._VisibleActions.ADD = true;
		fieldControl._VisibleActions.ADDURL = false;
		fieldControl._VisibleActions.DOCTYPE = false;
		fieldControl._VisibleAttributes.ENABLELINK = false;
		oComponent.setAttributes(fieldControl);

		oComponent.setMode("I");
	}.bind(this));
}

entry.title: Re: How to Assign Ressource to the Project
entry.content:
Hello @Ken_Melching,

could you please clarify how to differentiate between the two solution SAP PPM and PS and then how to assign resources on both solution.

Thanks a lot for your help.

entry.title: Re: Restrict only two records on Custom MDF Portlet
entry.content:
Hello @joe1989 ,

This might work.

You can add a new field with auto number as data type in the object lets say the label record counter. The expectation is that this field will get a number assigned to it for each bank record. Then you need to configure a business rule to raise an error message if the record counter is more than 2. 

Hope this helps.

Regards,

Surya

entry.title: Best Practice for Allocating Fixed Expenses in Administrative Areas – S/4HANA or SAC Planning?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I have a question regarding the best approach for distributing fixed expenses in administrative areas, both for budgeting and actuals.

  1. Budgeting: Is it recommended to handle the allocation logic within the budgeting module in SAP S/4HANA, or would it be more efficient to manage this in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) Planning?
  2. Actuals: If the allocation drivers are not recorded in the ERP, would it be better to perform the expense distribution in S/4HANA or directly in SAC Planning?

Looking forward to your insights and best practices.

Thanks!

SAP Analytics Cloud SAP S/4HANA Finance SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Re: Close Job Requisition: Workaround for No Warning Mess...
entry.content:
Hi @MarianeMelo ,

Potential Solutions to Address the Limitation of No Warning Message When Closing Job Requisitions in SuccessFactors Recruiting:

Customizing Notifications/Alerts in SAP SuccessFactors:

You can configure notifications or alerts through the Admin Center to remind users before they close a requisition. These alerts can be set to notify the hiring manager or recruiter, prompting them to confirm the action and review the requisition closure to avoid accidental submissions.

Custom Integrations or Enhancements in SuccessFactors:

With assistance from an Integration Consultant, you can create custom integrations or use SuccessFactors API and Extension Studio to trigger a confirmation prompt before closing a requisition. This approach involves more technical setup but offers a more tailored solution to fit your specific needs.
User Training & Best Practices:

If a technical solution isn't feasible, investing in user training and clear guidelines can help. Providing resources like checklists and detailed instructions on what needs to be checked before closing a requisition can significantly reduce the chances of mistakes.
Long-Term Solution:
For a more permanent fix, consider submitting a feature request to SAP via the SAP Influence Portal. If similar requests are received from other customers, SAP may consider adding a confirmation message or warning for closing requisitions in future product updates.

Hope this helps!!

Thanks,

kr,

karthick.

entry.title: Re: Everything you need to know about: SAP SuccessFactors Talent Intelligence Hub
entry.content: @AlexxBraggCTR  , Yes ! I suggest referring to my latest blogs (you will find in the community) that has more information.

entry.title: Pre-delivered templates not available in App ''Maintain Email Templates'
entry.content:
When calling the app 'Maintain Email Templates', in the pre-delivered tab, we cannot find the following templates for the quotation management:

  • New approval request: SD_SLS_QTN_APPROVAL_EMAIL_TMPL
  • Approval reminder: SD_SLS_QTN_DEADLINE_EMAIL_TMPL
  • Document release: SD_SLS_QTN_RELEASE_EMAIL_TMPL
  • Document rejection: SD_SLS_QTN_REJECT_EMAIL_TMPL
  • Document rework request: SD_SLS_QTN_REWORK_EMAIL_TMPL

Is there a way to upload them? Should we create them from scratch via SE80?

entry.title: Re: SBOP BI Platform 4.3 SP04 Patch 10: Logon failed for...
entry.content: You have to use the tomcat config tool. On Windows you can find it in your start menu.

entry.title: Re: SAC Excel add-in Convert to formula
entry.content: Unfortunately, the Excel Add-In is extremely undeveloped. Not sure why did they introduced it when it is only partially functional. Totally disappointed with this.

entry.title: Re: Katowice - SAP Stammtisch 23.04.2025
entry.content: UWAGA - przesuwamy spotkanie SAP Stammtisch na po Wielkanocy, do zobaczenia 🍺

entry.title: Re: Retail, Fashion and Vertical Business in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
This is significant for our customers looking to leverage SAP Business AI and SAP Joule - but also to have SAP's deep industry knowledge to be integrated into Joule, where a customer can now leverage industry performance benchmarking in real-time with SAP Joule.

 

SAP Business Suite  SAP Graph  SAP Business AI

entry.title: Re: Teams integrations affected by MC985483 (New default...
entry.content:
Hi there,

Currently, when setting up classes in the LMS, we do not explicitly define a value for the isPasscodeRequired flag. As a result, even if a passcode is set during meeting creation, users may not always be prompted to enter it.

With Teams' recent update, the default value of this flag is now set to true. However, since the LMS does not explicitly configure this flag, whether users see a passcode prompt will depend on their Single Sign-On (SSO) configuration. If SSO manages authentication, the passcode prompt may be bypassed.

Hope this helps!

entry.title: Re: SBOP BI Platform 4.3 SP04 Patch 10: Logon failed for...
entry.content: Great, thank you so much 👍

entry.title: Re: Moving Average Price changing after procurement of fr...
entry.content:
The moving price will change if you receive the material in the same valuation area and valuation type (if exists)-Best practice recommended by SAP- if the Vendor providing this material and your company now owns it then you would let the moving average price get changed as moving average price works on the concept of qty available and the amount available at time GR or IR. 

if you are looking for work around as suggested by others too 

1- You can use split valuation -where you can keep the price as 0.01 and receive the material in that valuation 

2. You can create another plant for receiving the goods but I would not recommend it as it will lead to redundant stock movements, data maintenance, and impact MRP or maybe your security roles 

3. you can create different materials--not different material types -- but again what are the advantages? as you have to do data maintenance, losing track of materials and also usability will be impacted 

Make sure you have pros and cons presented to your customer for each option and if you check with your customer finance team and convince your clients then you can save yourself all the work around. As this volume may be very less and impact on MAP may be minimal.

The recommendation is to receive this material as the main material so that you can save yourself and your client headaches later when it come to practically using this material. From FI and SCM point of view, there is nothing wrong with MAP price changes when you do free goods receipt.

entry.title: Re: Good practice for attributes setup for Business Criti...
entry.content:
Good day Vladimir,

Thank you for sharing your question on the SAP Community.  To provide support in regard to your question, 

 Please create a support case using SAP for Me (log in with S-User ID and password). 
You will need to select the specific SAP Signavio product / component as shown in the example below.
 
System: 
Search for SAP Signavio  
 
Product Area: 
SAP Signavio Process Manager > SAP Signavio Process Manager 
 
Component: 
BPI (Business Process Intelligence) 
---  
BPI-PI (SAP Signavio Process Insights) or 
BPI-SIG (SAP Business Transformation Suite by SAP Signavio) – Product & Topic 
 
For further information you can find more details in the SAP Signavio Onboarding Resource Center.
 
Kind regards,
KantriceD

entry.title: An earlier article on 'SAP Datasphere Security & Data Access Controls' receives an update
entry.content:
I have just updated my previous article titled "SAP Datasphere Security & Data Access Controls" with a few minor changes, one of which is quite significant. I’m pleased to announce that SAP now fully supports database users accessing views secured by Data Access Controls, as confirmed by SAP Product Management. Although this information is not yet documented, I wanted to share it with the community, as I believe many will find it important. My original article has been revised to include this update, and I’ve also summarized the details about this support here.

Additionally, I made several other minor updates in my original article to reflect recent product improvements.

 

Database users are eligible for Data Access Controls.

 

Data Access Controls can be enjoyed by all users

Regular application users

  • These are users who interact with Datasphere directly via the User Interface or consume it via an SAP Analytics Cloud ‘live’ connection.
  • These users exist in the Datasphere user directory and require authentication via the SAML Identity Provider

Database users

  • These are technical users connecting via a supported ODBC/JDBC
  • These users do not exist in the external identity directory, instead, they are authenticated by Datasphere directly and no SAML authentication is used.
  • An example database user is shown here. The username is ‘DATA_ACCESS_DEMO#MATTHEW’.

Although database user support for access control is not yet documented, rest assured that it is fully supported.

 

Setting Database Users for Data Access Controls.

Configuring Data Access Controls for database users is quite straightforward:

 

 

Step 1. Via Menu-Space Management-User Assignment-Database users-Create new database user

 

Step 2. Add the Database User Name to the Permissions table.

  • Shown here, ‘DATA_ACCESS_DEMO#MATTHEW’ is the database username added to the ‘Single Values permissions structure’ used earlier

 

Step 3. Access the view using the database user

  • Shown here with SAP HANA Database Explorer, we can see the database user has been restricted as expected, as defined in the permissions structure table
  • The ‘View_Single_Value_with_DAC’ already has a data access control defined on it as described in my earlier article and video

entry.title: Re: BI 2025 Countdown with Eric Fenollosa & Gregory Botticchio
entry.content:
@nachtaktiv Thanks for the question. Allow me to precise a few things : 

"it is a proprietary file-format and the user probably has to install some kind of software like the Client-Tools before to open and interact with this widx-file" >> No. Nothings is required to be installed before. The ".widx" file and its sub folder are autonomous. It contains everything to be executed on the end-user laptop/PC (mostly: .html files and .js files). 

"More important is the ability to export the complete report-structure/data as html-content/file(s) so the recipient can use it with nothing but a browser." >> And it is indeed how the .widx file works. The widx file contains data, report structure (layout + viz) and all the interactions. When you open the widx file, it will call a .bat file that will execute the Chromium kernel of your current browser, and will execute itself in it as an independent application.  

Hope this clarifies. 

GB

entry.title: Re: Input control unexpected behavior SAC
entry.content: @Yogeshwar_M I have the same issue after loading full data 😞

entry.title: Re: Handling Import Tariffs on Steel Products in SAP (USA...
entry.content:
This may not 100% helpful but You may able to handle it by creating new conditions % age and value as ZIDP or ZIDV-- as import duty % and value and then in the procedure you can assign FR3 key accruals --they way you would able to capture the amount in different G/L accounts and amount also get loaded on material when received. 

Another option could be adding some condition type in Tax codes and maintaining the condition -- I think t-codes are FTXP but you have to explore a little more on it.

I am not sure how you would address the sales side of import duty -it may be also adding a condition and adding the condition to the sales pricing procedure and account determination on the sales side if you need to offset the import duties as input and output tax way.

Also, i would recommend creating a case with SAP to know if they are releasing any KBA or Notes to address this requirement.

entry.title: Noreply Management - Address Not Found
entry.content:
Hi,

We have transactional email addresses starting with noreply@... There are several domains starting with noreply. How can I arrange what to happen if someone replies to these emails?

Thanks.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Noreply Management - Address Not Found
entry.content:
You can use the Reply Management tool to manage those addresses as well. Usually, most replies are considered "Unknown" type, but you can set up different replies based on each type and unknown as well.

If you would like to also send an email informing the customer that their reply won't be processed as it is a "noreply" email, you can set up an automation with the trigger Channel Engagement. You can read more about both features at the SAP Help articles below:

  • Reply management | SAP Help Portal
  • Channel Engagement | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Contribution View for Nutrients in SAP PLM Recipe Development
entry.content:
Hi Team,

In the PLM Recipe Development of HANA 1909 version, I have maintained 4 nutrients in a nutrient group and can see those values in (g) from nutrients tab under Input and output tab. Whereas same when I am trying to assign the layout of nutrients from the "Contribution" tab, my assigned nutrient values against my ingredients are showing up from this Contribution tab.

Requesting anyone to help me out on this issue. (Below is the screenshot for reference)

Your prompt response is much appreciated.

Thanks,

Abdul Kalhisha

entry.title: Step-By-Step Guide: Google Sheets API Integration in SAP CPI via Open Connectors
entry.content:
Introduction:

Google Sheets is widely used for real-time data management, but integrating it with SAP CPI can be challenging without the right approach. SAP Open Connectors simplifies this process by providing a pre-built, secure connection, eliminating the need for complex authentication and API configurations. In this blog, we’ll guide you through the step-by-step process of connecting Google Sheets with SAP CPI, enabling seamless data exchange.

 

Why Use SAP Open Connectors Instead of Direct Google Sheets API?

SAP Open Connectors simplifies Google Sheets integration with SAP CPI by providing a prebuilt, managed connection that eliminates the need for manual OAuth token handling and API endpoint configurations. It offers a standardized base URL for all API calls, making integration more consistent. Additionally, Open Connectors provides built-in API traffic monitoring, allowing you to track requests and responses in real time. Unlike direct integration, where you must manage authentication, API updates, and error handling, Open Connectors ensures seamless connectivity with minimal effort.

FactorSAP Open ConnectorsDirect Google Sheet API
AuthenticationPre-configured OAuth, no manual token handling.Requires implementing OAuth 2.0 authentication manually.
Prebuilt ConnectorReady to use, no API endpoint setup required.API requests must be built and maintained.
API Traffic MonitoringLogs API call history (method, URL, status) within Open Connectors.Requires custom logging setup in SAP CPI logs.
API MaintenanceSAP ensures API compatibility and updates.Must handle API changes manually.
Base URL ConsistencySingle, standardized base URL for all requests.Different endpoints for each API function.
Ease of UseMinimal coding effort, ready to use setup.Requires API knowledge and development effort.

 

Step-By-Step Guide:

Step 1: Enable Open Connectors in SAP Integration Suite

To use Open Connectors, Extend Non-SAP Connectivity capability should be activated in Integration Suite.

Click on Discover Connectors in Extend Non-SAP Connectivity

 

Step 2: Create a Google Sheets Instance in Open Connectors

Click on Instances

Click on Create Instance on the top right.

Select Google Sheets V4 

Click on Create Instance

A Google Sign-In screen will appear. Enter your Google account credentials.

If a security warning appears, click Advanced → Go to Cloud Element V2.

Grant the necessary permissions to access Google Sheets.

Once authenticated, the Google Sheets instance will be created

 

Step 3: Retrieve the Google Sheet ID

Open the Google Sheet you want to integrate. 

Look at the URL in the browser

https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/<sheet_id>/edit

Extract the Sheet ID from the URL

Example Sheet ID: 

1QqT7-E00EuILNsg4mkgsMdcKOycOmEblKaGE_s22Yw0

 

Step 4: Test Connection in Open Connectors

Open your Google Sheets instance in Open Connectors. 

Enter the Sheet ID and the Sheet Name.

Click Try It Out to test the connection.

You should receive a JSON response with the sheet data.

 

Step 5: Get Base URL & Authentication Details

Copy the Request URL from the API response

Extract the Base URL (everything up to /api-v2):

https://api.openconnectors.trial.us10.ext.hana.ondemand.com/elements/api-v2

Retrieve authentication details from Open Connectors Settings (bottom left corner): 

User: <User Secret>

Organization: <Organization Secret>  

Element (Instance Token): <Instance Token>

Create Credential in SAP CPI

Open Cloud Integration > Monitor > Security Material > User Credentials >  Type > Open Connectors

Step 6: Configure SAP CPI iFlow

Create an iFlow as below

Add Content Modifier and Set required Message Headers & Exchange Property

Message Headers

Exchange Property

Add Request Reply and Connect to Open Connectors Adapter

Go to Connection tab and add parameters that are saved in step 5.

Add Groovy Script to convert Google Sheets JSON response into XML 

 

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message;
import groovy.json.JsonSlurper;

def Message processData(Message message) {
 def body = message.getBody(String);
 def json = new JsonSlurper().parseText(body);

 def headers = json.values[0];
 def xmlOutput = new StringBuilder();
    xmlOutput.append("<Records>");

    json.values[1..- 1].each {
        row ->
        xmlOutput.append("<Record>");
        headers.eachWithIndex {
            col, idx ->
            def value = row[idx] ?: "";
            xmlOutput.append("<" + col + ">" + escapeXml(value) + "</" + col + ">");
        }
        xmlOutput.append("</Record>");
    }

    xmlOutput.append("</Records>");
    message.setBody(xmlOutput.toString());
    return message;
}

// Function to escape XML special characters
def String escapeXml(String input) {
    return input.replaceAll("&", "&")
        .replaceAll("<", "<")
        .replaceAll(">", ">")
        .replaceAll("\"", """)
        .replaceAll("'", "'")
}

 

Save and Deploy

Json Response from the Open Connector (Google Sheet API)

XML Data after conversion using groovy script

 

You can also explore other API methods by selecting the required resources from the List of Resources in the Connection tab of Open Connectors. This allows you to interact with different Google Sheets functionalities, including:

  • Fetching data from sheets, worksheets, specific rows or cell ranges (GET)
  • Creating new spreadsheets or worksheets (POST)
  • Updating existing sheet data (PUT)
  • Deleting rows, sheets or entire spreadsheets (DELETE)

By leveraging these options, you can perform both data retrieval and modifications directly through Open Connectors, making it a flexible solution for Google Sheets integration in SAP CPI.

 

Monitor API Traffic in Open Connectors

In Open Connectors, got to Activity and Select Google Sheet Instance.

Now you can see all the API logs for the instance.

You can track various data points such as:

  • API Request Details like Endpoint URL, HTTP Method, Request Headers, Query Parameters, Timestamp.
  • API Response Details like Status Code, Response Time.

 

Conclusion:

In this blog, we explored how SAP Open Connectors streamlines Google Sheets integration with SAP CPI by simplifying authentication, standardizing API interactions, and providing built-in monitoring. Unlike direct API integration, Open Connectors reduces complexity by handling token management and offering a unified API structure. Additionally, its diverse set of resources enables fetching, creating, updating, and deleting spreadsheet data seamlessly. By leveraging Open Connectors, you can enhance efficiency, ensure security, and build a more scalable integration with Google Sheets.

Let me know your thoughts or if you have any questions drop a comment below!

Happy Integrating!

entry.title: Re: Migration of Interview Scheduling using Microsoft Out...
entry.content:
@mfpabon- We received the same error when we tried enabling "Outlook and Teams for Online Meetings" in Work Tech! We had to go back to Outlook Integration only because Interview Scheduler was completely unusable. But because we removed the configuration, SAP Support could not replicate the error and closed the ticket. 

The transition back to Outlook Integration was not smooth either! I've spent the last 3-4 weeks trying to iron out all of the issues. Currently working on invite emails getting stuck in "Delivering" status.  

I look forward to reading about your experience and the experience of others who have successfully enabled "Outlook and Teams for Online Meetings" in Work Tech. 

Best of luck!
Tara

entry.title: Re: Are there any APIs to create a Purchase Requisition in Ariba
entry.content: Hi, Were you able to figure out how to do this? I have similar use case. Any help would be highly appreciated

entry.title: Re: PPDS user exit create order
entry.content: Hi, don't use BAdI. Use Data Replication Framework. Tcode DRFIMG. There you can setup the whole IDoc replication. In your case LOIPRO IDocs will be send at order release and production order get also a status that it was replicated. For PP-PI you need to do some extra setup.

entry.title: Rehire Co-Ordinator Group Assignment
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Is there a way to hide the compensation information for the Rehire Candidates in the cross entities?
Example, if the Onboardee belongs to Group A Entity, Group C Entity's Rehire Co-Ordinator should not be able to access the compensation information. How can we achieve this?
Else
Can we hide/grey out the Start button in the Employment Profile Selection Tab for Group C Entity's Rehire Co-Ordinator if the Candidate belongs Group A Entity?


Please let me know, thanks in advance.

entry.title: Deactivating Simplified Sourcing in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA introduced several simplifications in Production Planning, especially for Material Requirements Planning (MRP), as I explained in my blog MRP: Simplifications in S/4HANA. One of these simplifications is known as Simplified Sourcing, and it changes the logic used by MRP to determine the source of supply for a replenishment proposal with external procurement.

Some of the changes introduced by Simplified Sourcing in the MRP source determination were:

  • Source List is no longer mandatory for source determination.
  • Checking the new flag in “Auto Sourcing” in the info record is enough to make a source of supply relevant for MRP.
  • Contracts and Scheduling Agreements are automatically selected by MRP as a source of supply, if they exist.

SAP note 2268069 explains the simplified sourcing logic in detail, and it suggests that it is simpler than classic MRP because of the following reasons:

  • The different sources of supply are pretty symmetrical. Sources of supply have a temporal validity, a quantitative validity, a deletion indicator, and a "relevant for automatic sourcing" indicator. The sourcing logic can treat the different sources of supply in a uniform way
  • Quota arrangements can be used to prioritize any of the possible sources of supply
  • Purchasing info records are proper sources of supply. The SAP S/4HANA MRP can select purchasing info records without source list entries. It is sufficient to define the supplier information of a material in the purchasing info record.
  • Production versions make sure BOM and routing fit together
  • Production versions have always been the only in-house production source of supply integrated with APO PP/DS. With production versions as the only in-house production source of supply, ERP sourcing and PP/DS sourcing become more similar.
  • Purchasing info records, contracts, and delivery schedules have always been the external procurement sources of supply integrated with APO. With purchasing info records being proper sources of supply, ERP sourcing and PP/DS sourcing become more similar
  • A unified source of supply model is the basis for future innovations in sourcing

 

While simplified sourcing indeed streamlines the master data required for source determination, it brings one additional challenge to companies that are migrating from SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA, as they will need to review the existing master data, in order to make sure that source determination works in SAP S/4HANA.

In order to minimize the migration efforts, SAP implemented a new setting that allows simplified sourcing to be deactivated for external procurement. This new logic can be introduced into all the supported SAP S/4HANA releases with the following notes:

3552667 - Feature: Disable/Enable simplified sourcing in S/4HANA for plants - Dynpro changes
3493681 - Feature: Disable/Enable simplified sourcing in S/4HANA for plants.

SAP Note 3493681 introduces the new flag 'SKIP_SIMPLIFIED_SOURCING' in table T399D, and when this flag is checked, then only the source list is considered by both MRP and MRP Live as a valid source of supply.

SAP Note 3552667 implements the screen changes and it brings the field 'Skip Simplified Sourcing' into customizing. This flag can be accessed either through customizing transaction OMDT (external procurement customizing) or OPPQ (plant parameters customizing). Figure 1 shows the setting checked in view Default Values for Purchasing/MRP.

Figure 1: Skip Simplified Sourcing

 

 

Now let’s see some examples of how exactly this flag works in MRP and observe the MRP behavior with different combination of settings.

Example 1:

  • Flag Skip Simplified Sourcing Unchecked
  • Auto Sourcing Checked in the Info Record
  • No Source List

In this example we will just simulate the standard Simplified Sourcing logic, because the new flag Skip Simplified Sourcing is unchecked. Figure 2 shows that the flag Auto Sourcing is checked in the Info Record of supplier VENDOR_001 for material S4HANA_ROH_005.

Figure 2: Auto Sourcing checked in the Info Record

 

After running MRP, we can see in transaction that this supplier has been selected as a valid source of supply for a purchase requisition created by MRP, because the Simplified Sourcing logic was used by MRP.

Figure 3: Source of Supply determined by MRP

 

Now let’s go to the next example, where we will deactivate Simplified Sourcing and run MRP again, with the same master data.

Example 1:

  • Flag Skip Simplified Sourcing Checked
  • Auto Sourcing Checked in the Info Record
  • No Source List

In the new example we kept the same master data previously used, but we have checked the flag Skip Simplified Sourcing in the MRP customizing.

Figure 4: Skip Simplified Sourcing Checked in customizing

 

When we run MRP for the same material and with an identical planning situation, we can observe that MRP could not determine a vendor for the purchase requisition. That happened because we don’t have a source list for our material, and with the Simplified Sourcing logic deactivated, the Source List becomes mandatory for the MRP Source Determination.

Note that the system behavior will be the same if we run Classic MRP  or MRP Live.

 

Figure 5: Source of Supply was not determined by MRP, because there is no Source List

 

Now let’s see another example, where we will keep Simplified Sourcing deactivated and we will create a Source List.

Example 3:

  • Flag Skip Simplified Sourcing Checked
  • Auto Sourcing Checked in the Info Record for VENDOR_001
  • Source List created where VENDOR_002 is relevant to MRP.

In this third example we will keep the Skip Simplified Sourcing flag checked, and we will create a Source List, but the supplier relevant to MRP will be VENDOR_002, for which the Auto Sourcing flag is unchecked in the Info Record.

Figure 6: Source List with a valid Source of Supply

 

After we run MRP for our material, we can observe in MD04 that VENDOR_002 has been selected as the purchase requisition source of supply, as it is relevant for MRP in the source list.

Figure 7: Source of Supply determined by MRP

 

 

In the examples above we have demonstrated the source determination logic using only Info Records and Source Lists, but the results would be similar when using a Contract or Scheduling agreement:

  • If the Skip Simplified Sourcing is checked, Contracts and Scheduling Agreements will be automatically selected as a source of supply.
  • If the Skip Simplified Sourcing is unchecked, Contracts and Scheduling Agreements will only be selected as a source of supply if there is a source list entry determining that they should be selected by MRP.

 

 

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG

entry.title: Localising links in Email Recommender block
entry.content:
How do we use localised links in the Email Recommender block?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Localising links in Email Recommender block
entry.content:
According to the SAP Emarsys Help document linked below, the Link To field works based on the localized fields that are in place at your product catalog:

  • Link to – Set a link to the selected Product Catalog field from the drop-down list. The localized link field will be referenced in the widget rendering template when displaying a recommender widget for visitors who are looking at, for example the Spanish version of the website. For more information, see field localization.

SAP Emarsys Help: Adding recommendations to your emails

The usage of this field is to create a different email recommender block/widget for each of the localization region. Then, system will be able to find the link to the correct product region/language will be selected by the system when creating and sending the email.

entry.title: Re: Erro gravação NF no SAP
entry.content: O suporte da SAP não conseguiu resolver, tivemos que devolver o documento.

entry.title: Re: Cancelar Nota de Saída (Nenhum registro concordante e...
entry.content: O suporte da SAP não conseguiu resolver, tivemos que devolver o documento para, pelo menos, corrigir os saldos na contabilidade.

entry.title: Propose IBAN in Bank Master Data of Business Partner during Data Migration or Mass Change
entry.content:
Hello,

Is it possible to generate the IBAN for a multiple selection of Business Partners at once in the S/4Hana Cloud, Public Edition?

Via Data Migration, it is only possible to directly enter the IBAN. We would like to generate those automatically with the Data Migration or do a Mass Change - is this possible? Or do I always have to click manually at the propose IBAN button for each Business Partner?

 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Your Renewals are now Available on SAP for Me
entry.content: is there a difference between the renewal data in the customer success tab vs that in the finance and legal tab? are they the same?

entry.title: SFTP File Transfer: Sending Attachments Securely in SAP CPI
entry.content:
Software Needed

  1. SFTP server details if you have, else we will create a SFTP free server.
  2. FileZilla installed in your system to check the server files.

Steps:

  1. Go to https://sftpcloud.io/tools/free-sftp-server website & create a free SFTP server

2. Create a new instance.

3. Set a name and Storage for new instance and create it.

4.

5. Now installed FileZilla

  • Give Host Name, Username, Password, Port No

  • Then we have to check the status After Quick Connect

6. Open Tenant

Monitor ---> Overview ---> Test Connectivity

Get the host key and Copy & Paste in notepad file and save it.

Monitor ---> Overview ---> Manage Security Material ---> Security Material

Now Upload Your Host Key (that you saved in notepad)

Now go to Create à User Credentials

Enter the details

7. Create Artifact in which we need to pull the Attachment from SFTP server

8.  Configure SFTP

 

Add the directory path that you created in FileZilla

Address – Host Name

Credentials – In Monitor à User Credentials (that you created above)

9. Configure Mail

In Address: smtp.gmail.com:465

Protection: SMTPS

Then in processing we have Body Mime-Type

  • This defines the format of the email body.
  • Available options:
    • Application/JSON → Sends email body as JSON.
    • Application/Octet-Stream → Sends as raw binary data.
    • Application/X-GZip → Sends the body as a compressed GZIP file.
    • Application/XML → Sends data in XML format.
    • Application/ZIP → Sends the body as a ZIP file.
    • Text/CSV → Sends the body as a CSV file.
    • Text/HTML → Formats the email in HTML.
    • Text/Plain → Sends the email as plain text (default).

10. Postman

 

Used Postman to test an SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) API endpoint. The request was made using the GET method to the following URL:

 

Endpoint URL: https://229trail.it-cpitrial03-rt.cfapps.ap21.hana.ondemand.com/http/xml/test  

 

 

After sending the request, I received a 200 OK response, which indicates a successful connection.

 

Thank You,

Pranav Sharma

entry.title: Fiori Apps for searching Productions orders with SPLIT status
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Do we have any Standard Fiori Apps to display production orders which have undergone splitting.

Similar to COOIS where System Status SPLT gives list of orders which have splitted.

Manage Production Orders App is good Fiori App but doesn't display or give filter /column option to narrow down the search. One has to individually check the order Display Related Orders

Regards

ND

#SplitProductionOrders

entry.title: Re: HTML Application is not appearing in the HTML Application section in SAP BTP
entry.content:
Hi @durgaprasanth_vemula, 

This is the technology blog space. You should post your question on the technology Q&A space below instead:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/qa-p/technology-questions

entry.title: Re: Restrict Access Request creation for a particular Con...
entry.content:
Hi Rajesh,

 

I would suggest to perform two elements:

  • Please try to remove ECC2 connector from integration scenarios from AUTH, PROV and ROLMG, keeping only SUPMG as part of the configuration. But please do some additional testing because if I recall this might impact EAM functionality and additional SAP Notes to be implemented (such as 3421686 - Extend Lock/Unlock functionality for SUPMG scenario usage and others)
  • Run GRAC_DELETE_ACCESS_RULES to remove sync data from ECC2 connector (with option Delete Synchronization data for Physical systems). This program is explained in the mentioned SAP Note: 2268795 - GRAC_DELETE_ACCESS_RULES explained

But please be aware that you had to double-check system behaviour before running this on your production system.

 

Regards,

Lukasz

entry.title: Need Order Details of a Contact
entry.content:
Hi Team,

 

We need details of all the orders a specific contact has ever placed to investigate some automations that they were not supposed to enter.

 

Thanks and Regards.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Need Order Details of a Contact
entry.content:
Since you would like to extract the data of a single contact, you can do it by using the option "Start Contact Data Export" at the contact profile. This procedure is described at SAP Emarsys Help: Exporting data change history for all fields:

On the Contact Data Export tab, click Start Change History Export. Collecting all the data changes may take several minutes to complete. In the meantime, you can navigate from this page. Once the data export is ready to download you receive a notification.

Click the download link to save a .zip archive file to your computer containing a .csv file with all the change event records.

entry.title: Re: comment tab in grc screen throwing error
entry.content:
Hi @Adani_Basis_Lead,

 

Could you please check whether there are two elements:

  • are there any authorization issues happening on the approver level - this can be displayed via STAUTHTRACE (of course, assuming that you have already activated auth trace)
  • please check whether you have an LTXT value on the SE75 tcode level (mentioned in: 1801435 - SE75 - Text object usage in GRC application). This is not directly connected with your issue but based on my experience it might result in some strange behaviour in system way of working for GRC and your case might follow this example

Regards,

Lukasz

entry.title: É possível vincular os pedidos de compras à notas fiscais/recebimentos já lançados?
entry.content:
Boa tarde, colegas

Por um erro na integração de um add-on, alguns documentos de entrada (nota/recebimento) acabaram sendo lançados sem o vínculo com o pedido de compras. Existe algum meio de vincular os documentos?

Sistema: SAP Business One 9.3 - 9.30.230 PL 13

entry.title: Subcontracting to a subcontractor as a production order external operation
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have below scenario in MTO

Sales order is created, from which we have created a production order where we have one production operation ( external ) to send the semi finished material to a subcontractor, where a subcontractor will perform an operation and send that material to a customer directly .

Here ,do we need to maintain subcontractor as a storage location ? And from this operation, do we need to create a purchase requisition , followed by a po to capture the cost ? Will be thankful if someone can shed the light .

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud File Repository API
entry.content:
This support only READ (HTTP GET).  Perhapps you are looking at transport content permission here to view/edit..

api/v1/content | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: How to calculate difference between two dates in sap time schema
entry.content:
Hi All,

Could you please help me with the below issue?

Vacation accrual amounts are calculated based on the vacation eligibility date indicated in IT0041. However, these amounts are generated every anniversary based on the employee's start date rather than the vacation eligibility date.

For example, if an employee's start date is June 16, 2019, and their vacation eligibility date is November 26, 2003, the expected result is that the vacation quota should be generated on the anniversary of November 26, 2020.

Currently, the system uses a time type to accumulate  days from the start date. In the above example, the system accumulates time from June 16, 2019, resulting in 365 days by June 16, 2020. At that point, the quota is generated, and the time type is reset to 1 to begin counting for the next year.

However, in this scenario, the time type should start accumulating from November 26, 2019. Could you please suggest how to achieve this in the Personnel Calculation Rules (PCR)?

 

Thank you so much for your help in advance!

entry.title: Notable Changes in SAP Sales Cloud Version 2
entry.content:
1. Changes to Appointment Scheduling Notes in SAP Sales Cloud version 2

We want to inform you about an important update to the appointment scheduling feature in SAP Sales Cloud Version 2

Planned deployment date: the last week of March or the first week of April (dates subject to change)

What’s Changing?

We are introducing an enhanced Notes feature, which will replace the existing notes field with New Notes on the user interface (UI). This update allows for better organization and usability by enabling two distinct types of notes.

Key Benefits:

  • Note Differentiation – Separate internal comments from customer information notes for better clarity and management.
  • Expanded Character Limit – The previous 500-character limit has been removed, allowing for more detailed notes.
  • Outlook Syncing – Only customer information notes will sync to Outlook, ensuring internal comments remain private.
  • Customizable Note Types – Users can configure custom note types as per their specific needs.
  • Enhanced UI Experience – Notes will now follow a structured format similar to Visit/Account objects.

Impact on Existing Customers

This update includes a one-time migration of existing notes:

  • Existing notes will be moved to the internal comments section of the new Notes field.
  • API Path Change – If your integrations rely on the current API path, updates may be required.
  • Change History Tracking – The movement of notes will be recorded under the Change History tab for transparency.

Required Actions

If you have customized the Notes functionality, please review the following:

  • Adaptation/Personalization:
    • If any notes sections were hidden via adaptation or personalization, these settings may be impacted.
    • You may need to reapply changes through the adaptation screen.
  • Custom Validations, Determinations & External Hooks:
    • Settings → Extensibility Admin → Appointment Service
    • Any custom logic applied to the old notes field (e.g., validations, rules, or external integrations) will no longer work.
    • Please update the logic via:
  • Auto-Flow Rules:
    • Existing auto-flow rules based on the old notes/content field will not execute anymore.
    • You will need to update these rules to reflect the new Notes structure.

2. Changes to Individual Customer in SAP Sales Cloud Version 2

We want to inform you about an important update to the Individual Customer in SAP Sales Cloud Version 2

Planned deployment date: the first  week of March 2025

 What’s Changing?

The Individual customer is being added to the Sales scope (previously this was limited to only those Sales loud V2 tenants which also had Service Cloud V2.)

With this we also added to the Individual customer details UI the corresponding sales functionality:

  • Ability to create appointment, lead, opportunity, quote, task for the Individual customer
  • Sales history tab with leads, opportunities, quotes
  • Activity history tab with appointments

This will be available in all those tenants which have SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 scoped.

 Key Benefits:

  • Adapts sales processes - to different customer needs, making it easier to handle both B2B and B2C interactions.
  • Direct engagement - with individual customers allows for better relationship-building

Impact on Existing Customers

In order to enable this users will need to be assigned the business service sap.crm.md.service.individualCustomerService to the relevant business roles to activate Individual Customers.

If some users already have visibility to the Individual customers today, then they may see the following changes in the details view:

  • Create options for – Lead, Opportunity, Appointments, Sales Quotes, Sales Orders and Tasks (currently only Case is supported)
  • Additional Tabs – New tabs for Sales History and Activity History (similar to what is already available in the Accounts)

Required Actions

If you have customized the UI layout for users who already use the Individual customers please review the following:

  • Adaptation/Personalization:
    • If any tabs are needed to be hidden via adaptation or personalization, these settings may be impacted.
    • You may need to reapply changes through the adaptation screen.

 

Next Steps

We recommend reviewing your configurations ahead of the rollout to avoid disruptions. If you have any questions or require assistance, please reach out to the support team.

entry.title: Integrate Linkedin learning with Development Goals
entry.content:
Hello everyone, we have been working on enabling Learning Activity in the Development Module. This way the EE and Mgr can assign Linkedin Learning courses to the EE's specific Development Goal. In the Learning Library, there are free courses and courses with a cost that require the manager's approval. We need to find an option for Managers and EEs to get notified when the course assigned has a cost and requires approval.  

We tested the learning plan notification automatic process, this allows the system to send emails with the learning updates, courses that are assigned, and course completion due dates. Unfortunately, this solution involved all courses assigned in the Learning module, including the free courses, and even courses that were assigned in an early stage and wouldn’t be taken until later. This caused users to get emails irrespective of completions from the historic assignments, which differs from our scope.

I wanted to see if any other companies are dealing with this same issue and what their workaround is.

Thanks!

entry.title: LMS Search for Library-No libraries were found with the provided search criteria.
entry.content:
Hello,

We have a learning admin who created an Item and try to add that Item to their Library that they have access, but received a message "No libraries were found with the provided search criteria" when search for the library to add to the Item.  Also, when searching for the specific Library still no results.  I've checked the permission and the role assigned  to the Admin does have permission to do all these tasks.

Have anyone have this same issue? and what is the resolution if any.

Thank you in advance for your help!

Tam
